rag_rules: Assumptions and guarantees for component ports must be placed in component type scopes, not implementation scopes.
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections.
generic_rules: Use visible feature names and named constants in AGREE contracts when specifying bounds or relations.
Ensure helper declarations match the actual types of expressions used in assumptions and guarantees.
Subcomponent ports should not be referenced in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts in component types should use named constants or visible feature names for bounds.
Helper declarations for range checks should use boolean expressions combining comparison operators.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-specific bindings in component type scopes.
Do not reference subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts without proper variable representation.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE annex placement in system implementations
